title: Fermented Croatian Fish Stew
description: A festive, fermented fish stew with a glassy-jelly consistency, featuring cured cod, shio-koji cure, and a splash of white wine, perfect for a Dutch-oven celebratory meal.
instructions: In a Dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté diced red onion until translucent.
Add minced garlic and cook for an additional minute until fragrant.
Pour in white wine, stir, and let reduce by half.
Stir in shio-koji, cured cod, and fish stock; bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover and simmer for 15 minutes, allowing flavors to meld and the fish to cook through.
Add lemon zest, black pepper, and chopped dill; stir gently.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and let stew simmer for an additional 10 minutes to develop a slightly glassy, jelly-like consistency.
Taste and season with sea salt, then serve hot straight from the Dutch oven.
